items.headline: US pauses military operation in Strait of Hormuz for Iran talks
items.body: The United States paused its new mission to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz. This de-escalation creates a window for diplomacy to end the conflict with Iran.

items.headline: Romania's pro-EU government toppled in no-confidence vote
items.body: Romania's pro-European coalition government collapsed after losing a no-confidence vote in parliament. This triggers political turmoil in a key eastern European nation amid regional tensions.

items.headline: Modi's party wins control of West Bengal for the first time
items.body: India's ruling BJP has won state elections in West Bengal, an opposition stronghold. The victory consolidates the prime minister's power and further weakens the national opposition.

items.headline: Boko Haram militants kill 23 soldiers in attack on Chad military post
items.body: Boko Haram fighters attacked a military post in Chad’s Lake Chad region, killing soldiers. The assault signals a resurgence, threatening stability for countries in the entire region.

items.headline: China fireworks factory explosion kills 26 workers in Hunan Province
items.body: An explosion at a fireworks facility in Liuyang killed 26 and injured 61 on May 5. All fireworks manufacturers in the city have been ordered to halt production pending investigation.

items.headline: Romania coalition government collapses after no-confidence vote
items.body: Parliament removed Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan with 281 votes on May 6. Markets fell on concern the political crisis threatens deficit reduction pledges to Brussels.

items.headline: Trump pauses Strait of Hormuz operation one day after launch
items.body: The U.S. halted ship escort operations on May 6 citing progress toward Iran agreement. Six Iranian boats were sunk during the single day of military confrontation.

items.headline: Philippines declares national energy emergency as gasoline prices double
items.body: The government invoked emergency powers after fuel costs surged past previous highs. Indonesia and Vietnam have begun energy rationing in response to the regional supply shock.
